theme: Carving Runes to Odin for Victory (Sigr)
value: At Dusk, the practitioner stands at the Waterfall to honor Odin, the High One. Performing Carving Runes with an offering of Runestone, they seek Victory (Sigr). This rite invokes the god's power over wisdom, aligning the worshiper's wyrd with divine will.

To appease the restless dead (to appease), the Draugr, one goes to the Burial Mound (Haug) at Thing-tide. An offering of iron is left while performing Sumbel (Ritual Toasting). This ensures Appeasement and maintains balance with the unseen.

To appease the spirits of the land, the Landvaettir, one goes to the Longship Deck at Thing-tide. An offering of Lock of Hair is left while performing Seidr (Trance Magic). This ensures Curse (Agitation) and maintains balance with the unseen.

theme: Seidr (Trance Magic) to Hel for Curse (Niding)
value: At Sigrblót (Summer), the practitioner stands at the Burial Mound (Haug) to honor Hel, Daughter of Loki. Performing Seidr (Trance Magic) with an offering of Amber Bead, they seek Curse (Niding). This rite invokes the god's power over underworld, aligning the worshiper's wyrd with divine will.
During New Moon, it is wise to honor the Landvaettir with Libation. Placing Nail Clippings at the Horg (Stone Altar) invites Luck and wards off ill-luck.
For Protection, the householder connects with the Disir by offering ale at the Wetland/Bog. This act of Sumbel (Ritual Toasting) strengthens the bond with the female ancestral spirits.
A quiet ritual of Hanging Offerings for the Husvaettir. Offering Gold Armband at the Mountain Peak seeks their aid for Hearth Protection.

theme: Sumbel (Ritual Toasting) to Freyja for Prophecy
value: At Thing-tide, the practitioner stands at the Waterfall to honor Freyja, Wearer of Brisingamen. Performing Sumbel (Ritual Toasting) with an offering of Honey, they seek Prophecy. This rite invokes the god's power over beauty, aligning the worshiper's wyrd with divine will.

A rite of Healing dedicated to Baldr. The faithful gather at the Burial Mound (Haug) and offer Animal Blood (symbolic). through Blót (Sacrifice), they petition the Best of Gods to grant favor in matters of light.

theme: Seidr (Trance Magic) to Odin for Victory (Sigr)

During Dusk, it is wise to honor the Husvaettir with Lighting Sacred Fire. Placing Mead at the Ancient Oak Tree invites Luck and wards off ill-luck.

theme: Carving Runes to Eir for Medicine
value: To gain Medicine, one visits the Wetland/Bog during Winter Nights (Vetrnætr). An offering of Mugwort is presented to Eir while performing Carving Runes. By calling upon Eir as Handmaiden of Frigg, the devotee ensures their request is heard.

theme: Seidr (Trance Magic) to Njord for Safe Travel
value: To gain Safe Travel, one visits the Cave Entrance during Full Moon. An offering of Broken Weapon is presented to Njord while performing Seidr (Trance Magic). By calling upon Njord as Ship-Dweller, the devotee ensures their request is heard.
During Winter Nights (Vetrnætr), it is wise to honor the Alfar with Blót (Sacrifice). Placing Wildflowers at the Lund (Sacred Grove) invites Ancestral Connection and wards off ill-luck.
A quiet ritual of Heitstrenging (Vow-making) for the Fylgja. Offering Runestone at the Hearth seeks their aid for Protection.

theme: Sumbel (Ritual Toasting) to Eir for Medicine

value: During Thing-tide, it is wise to honor the Husvaettir with Lighting Sacred Fire. Placing bread at the Lund (Sacred Grove) invites Hearth Protection and wards off ill-luck.

To appease the spirits of the house, the Husvaettir, one goes to the Mountain Peak at Midsummer. An offering of bread is left while performing Hanging Offerings. This ensures Luck and maintains balance with the unseen.

To appease the spirits of the house, the Husvaettir, one goes to the Ancient Oak Tree at Dawn. An offering of Mead is left while performing Carving Runes. This ensures Peace (Frith) and maintains balance with the unseen.
